summary: Top 6 +EV picks · NBA Playoffs (Knicks-Hawks · Celtics-76ers · Nuggets-Wolves)

picks.reasoning: The model projects KAT for a median of 18.9 points, but the distribution is wide (p75 = 24.5). Sportsbook RI is offering +360 on the Over 24.5 — implying just 21.7% win probability — while our model puts it at 24.9%. That 3.2-point gap in implied probability generates +14.6% EV. RI is the market-best for this line (nearest is Hard Rock at +350). In a high-leverage playoff spot where NYK needs KAT healthy and producing, his scoring variance is wide enough to hit 25+ in roughly 1 in 4 games.

Embiid's median projection is 27.6 points, but the model's standard deviation is ~11.2 pts — reflecting his well-known boom-or-bust ceiling. RI is offering +310 on the Over 34.5, implying 24.4% probability, while our model puts it at 26.9%. That 2.5-point edge generates +10.3% EV. PHI needs a monster Embiid performance to stay competitive against Boston, and when he locks in against soft interior defense, 35+ point explosions are well within his range. RI's +310 beats the market consensus of +280–300.

A speculative alternate-line play, but a genuine +EV opportunity. Jokic's median assist projection is 9.9, but variance is wide (p75 = 12.6 assists). RI's +800 implies just 11.1% probability; our model places the true probability at 12.1% — a meaningful gap in a high-price market. DraftKings, the closest comparable, has this at +680, so RI is offering a substantial premium. In a high-pace game (225-pt total projected), Jokic facilitating at an elite level is a real scenario. Best played as a small unit given the probability. Note: this pick is correlated with Pick #4 — both cap out if Jokic underperforms.

The higher-probability companion to Pick #3 (correlated plays — take both or pick one). Jokic Over 11.5 assists at RI's +210 is the best price available anywhere in the market (FanDuel has +196, Caesars +159). The model puts 34.5% probability on 12+ assists; market implies 32.3% at +210 — a 2.2-point edge generating +7.0% EV. MIN will force DEN to run offense through Jokic all night in a must-win spot, keeping his playmaking opportunities elevated throughout. A 12-assist game is well within his range on any given night.

Tatum's median projection is 25.0 points with a wide distribution (σ ≈ 9.6 pts, p75 = 31.4). At +230, RI is tied with FanDuel for the market's best price on this alternate line — implying 30.3% probability against our model's 31.8%. A 1.5-point edge in implied probability generates +4.9% EV. BOS is a 6.5-point favorite, which sometimes means Tatum gets fewer late-game touches, but his shot-creation ability in playoff environments means a 30+ point explosion is always in play. Best price in the market for this line.

Brunson's median is 27.1 points but his variance is wide (σ ≈ 10.2 pts). RI's +340 is the clear market best — DraftKings has only +291, Hard Rock is +325. The model puts 23.5% probability on a 35+ point game vs the market's implied 22.7% at +340, generating +3.4% EV. Brunson has shown the ability to go for 40+ in playoff situations, and ATL's perimeter defense ranks in the bottom third of the league. Thinnest edge in today's slate but still clearly +EV at the right price.
